calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-28-2009 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-07-2009 Continued To Oct-28-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jul-28-2009 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Shustoff for and with Gertrude Lewis-Wayne (Administrator).

Mr. Shustoff stated that the Petition For Final Distribution was ready to be filed and that he received waivers from all Parties except for Valerie Wayne. The matter was taken off calendar.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley, CSR 225. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jul-08-2009 Continued To Jul-28-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearance: Mark Shustoff for and with Gertrude Lewis-Wayne.

The Court inquired on the status of the real property. Mr. Shustoff stated that the property had been re-appraised, that all the beneficiaries were in agreement as set forth on the record, that he has received one consent so far, and that Valerie Wayne is in agreement and she is present in court today. The Court continued the matter to July 28, 2009 at 9:00 a.m. for status on the petition for final distribution.

Judge: David Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar May-27-2009 Continued To Jul-08-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Shustoff for and with Gertrude Lewis-Wayne.

The Court stated that it has had an aquaintance with Mr. Shustoff for some time, that Ms. Wayne was a friend of the falily and that there was no need to disqualify himself as he would be fair and impartial to the matter. Furthermore, the Court stated that Probate Code 12200 required the Personal Representative petition for Final Distribution before one year of the date of death. Mr. Shustoff stated that his Client's Daughter was occupying the property, that his Client declined to sell the home, that the Daughter and his Client wanted to purchase the home and that his Client was in touch with two lenders. The Court stated that the interest of the other Beneficiaries needed to be taken into consideration and that if all of the Parties did not agree, that the property would need to be sold. The matter was continued to July 8, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to report that the property was listed on the market or for a written agreement between all Parties concerning the property.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar May-13-2009 Continued To May-27-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
